title: Crispy Uruguayan Renkon Lotus Root Empanadas
description: A low-calorie Uruguayan dish featuring renkon lotus root, this recipe combines bitter and flaky elements to create a show-off empanada, using mantecare emulsification for a unique taste experience.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, salt, and pepper.
Add cold butter to the bowl and use a pastry blender to work it into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
In a small bowl, whisk together eggs and water, then add the mixture to the flour mixture and stir until a dough forms.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
In a bowl, mix together diced renkon lotus root and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Place a tablespoon of the lotus root mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half over the filling and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with olive oil and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
